decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.27°C) exceeds the 85.0°C limit. The most effective primary lever is to increase the heat spreader conductivity to enhance lateral heat dissipation away from the chip. A conservative 40% increase is proposed, staying well within the allowed 2x step ratio and material bounds.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity improves lateral heat spreading, reducing the thermal gradient and peak temperature at the chip location.

expected_effects: More uniform temperature distribution across the spreader layer
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate or boundary conditions rather than the spreader itself.
High conductivity materials may be cost-prohibitive in real-world applications, though this is a simulation study.
